speaker: ORSINO
text:
Give me some music.
                 
                
                  Music
                   
                  plays
                  .
                
               Now, good morrow, friends.—

speaker: ORSINO
text: Now, good Cesario, but that piece of song,

speaker: ORSINO
text: That old and antique song we heard last night.

speaker: ORSINO
text: Methought it did relieve my passion much,

speaker: ORSINO
text: More than light airs and recollected terms

speaker: ORSINO
text: Of these most brisk and giddy-pacèd times.

speaker: ORSINO
text: Come, but one verse.

speaker: CURIO
text: He is not here, so please your Lordship, that

speaker: CURIO
text: should sing it.

speaker: ORSINO
text: Who was it?

speaker: CURIO
text: Feste the jester, my lord, a Fool that the Lady

speaker: CURIO
text: Olivia’s father took much delight in. He is about

speaker: CURIO
text: the house.

speaker: ORSINO
text:
Seek him out
                 
                Curio
                 
                exits
                ,
               and play the tune the while.

speaker: ORSINO
text:
To
                 
                Viola
                .
                 
              Come hither, boy. If ever thou shalt love,

speaker: ORSINO
text: In the sweet pangs of it remember me,

speaker: ORSINO
text: For such as I am, all true lovers are,

speaker: ORSINO
text: Unstaid and skittish in all motions else

speaker: ORSINO
text: Save in the constant image of the creature

speaker: ORSINO
text: That is beloved. How dost thou like this tune?

speaker: VIOLA
text: It gives a very echo to the seat

speaker: VIOLA
text: Where love is throned.

speaker: ORSINO
text: Thou dost speak masterly.

speaker: ORSINO
text: My life upon ’t, young though thou art, thine eye

speaker: ORSINO
text: Hath stayed upon some favor that it loves.

speaker: ORSINO
text: Hath it not, boy?

speaker: VIOLA
text: A little, by your favor.

speaker: ORSINO
text: What kind of woman is ’t?

speaker: VIOLA
text: Of your complexion.

speaker: ORSINO
text: She is not worth thee, then. What years, i’ faith?

speaker: VIOLA
text: About your years, my lord.

speaker: ORSINO
text: Too old, by heaven. Let still the woman take

speaker: ORSINO
text: An elder than herself. So wears she to him;

speaker: ORSINO
text: So sways she level in her husband’s heart.

speaker: ORSINO
text: For, boy, however we do praise ourselves,

speaker: ORSINO
text: Our fancies are more giddy and unfirm,

speaker: ORSINO
text: More longing, wavering, sooner lost and worn,

speaker: ORSINO
text: Than women’s are.

speaker: VIOLA
text: I think it well, my lord.

speaker: ORSINO
text: Then let thy love be younger than thyself,

speaker: ORSINO
text: Or thy affection cannot hold the bent.

speaker: ORSINO
text: For women are as roses, whose fair flower,

speaker: ORSINO
text: Being once displayed, doth fall that very hour.

speaker: VIOLA
text: And so they are. Alas, that they are so,

speaker: VIOLA
text: To die even when they to perfection grow!

speaker: ORSINO
text: O, fellow, come, the song we had last night.—

speaker: ORSINO
text: Mark it, Cesario. It is old and plain;

speaker: ORSINO
text: The spinsters and the knitters in the sun

speaker: ORSINO
text: And the free maids that weave their thread with bones

speaker: ORSINO
text: Do use to chant it. It is silly sooth,

speaker: ORSINO
text: And dallies with the innocence of love

speaker: ORSINO
text: Like the old age.

speaker: FOOL
text: Are you ready, sir?

speaker: ORSINO
text: Ay, prithee, sing.

speaker: FOOL
text: Come away, come away, death,

speaker: FOOL
text: And in sad cypress let me be laid.

speaker: FOOL
text: Fly away, fly away, breath,

speaker: FOOL
text: I am slain by a fair cruel maid.

speaker: FOOL
text: My shroud of white, stuck all with yew,

speaker: FOOL
text: O, prepare it!

speaker: FOOL
text: My part of death, no one so true

speaker: FOOL
text: Did share it.

speaker: FOOL
text: Not a flower, not a flower sweet

speaker: FOOL
text: On my black coffin let there be strown;

speaker: FOOL
text: Not a friend, not a friend greet

speaker: FOOL
text: My poor corpse where my bones shall be thrown.

speaker: FOOL
text: A thousand thousand sighs to save,

speaker: FOOL
text: Lay me, O, where

speaker: FOOL
text: Sad true lover never find my grave

speaker: FOOL
text: To weep there.

speaker: ORSINO
text: There’s for thy pains.

speaker: FOOL
text: No pains, sir. I take pleasure in singing, sir.

speaker: ORSINO
text: I’ll pay thy pleasure, then.

speaker: FOOL
text: Truly sir, and pleasure will be paid, one time or

speaker: FOOL
text: another.

speaker: ORSINO
text: Give me now leave to leave thee.

speaker: FOOL
text: Now the melancholy god protect thee and the

speaker: FOOL
text: tailor make thy doublet of changeable taffeta, for thy

speaker: FOOL
text: mind is a very opal. I would have men of such

speaker: FOOL
text: constancy put to sea, that their business might be

speaker: FOOL
text: everything and their intent everywhere, for that’s it

speaker: FOOL
text: that always makes a good voyage of nothing.

speaker: FOOL
text: Farewell.

speaker: ORSINO
text: Let all the rest give place.

speaker: ORSINO
text: Once more, Cesario,

speaker: ORSINO
text: Get thee to yond same sovereign cruelty.

speaker: ORSINO
text: Tell her my love, more noble than the world,

speaker: ORSINO
text: Prizes not quantity of dirty lands.

speaker: ORSINO
text: The parts that Fortune hath bestowed upon her,

speaker: ORSINO
text: Tell her, I hold as giddily as Fortune.

speaker: ORSINO
text: But ’tis that miracle and queen of gems

speaker: ORSINO
text: That nature pranks her in attracts my soul.

speaker: VIOLA
text: But if she cannot love you, sir—

speaker: ORSINO
text: I cannot be so answered.

speaker: VIOLA
text: Sooth, but you must.

speaker: VIOLA
text: Say that some lady, as perhaps there is,

speaker: VIOLA
text: Hath for your love as great a pang of heart

speaker: VIOLA
text: As you have for Olivia. You cannot love her;

speaker: VIOLA
text: You tell her so. Must she not then be answered?

speaker: ORSINO
text: There is no woman’s sides

speaker: ORSINO
text: Can bide the beating of so strong a passion

speaker: ORSINO
text: As love doth give my heart; no woman’s heart

speaker: ORSINO
text: So big, to hold so much; they lack retention.

speaker: ORSINO
text: Alas, their love may be called appetite,

speaker: ORSINO
text: No motion of the liver but the palate,

speaker: ORSINO
text: That suffer surfeit, cloyment, and revolt;

speaker: ORSINO
text: But mine is all as hungry as the sea,

speaker: ORSINO
text: And can digest as much. Make no compare

speaker: ORSINO
text: Between that love a woman can bear me

speaker: ORSINO
text: And that I owe Olivia.

speaker: VIOLA
text: Ay, but I know—

speaker: ORSINO
text: What dost thou know?

speaker: VIOLA
text: Too well what love women to men may owe.

speaker: VIOLA
text: In faith, they are as true of heart as we.

speaker: VIOLA
text: My father had a daughter loved a man

speaker: VIOLA
text: As it might be, perhaps, were I a woman,

speaker: VIOLA
text: I should your Lordship.

speaker: ORSINO
text: And what’s her history?

speaker: VIOLA
text: A blank, my lord. She never told her love,

speaker: VIOLA
text: But let concealment, like a worm i’ th’ bud,

speaker: VIOLA
text: Feed on her damask cheek. She pined in thought,

speaker: VIOLA
text: And with a green and yellow melancholy

speaker: VIOLA
text: She sat like Patience on a monument,

speaker: VIOLA
text: Smiling at grief. Was not this love indeed?

speaker: VIOLA
text: We men may say more, swear more, but indeed

speaker: VIOLA
text: Our shows are more than will; for still we prove

speaker: VIOLA
text: Much in our vows but little in our love.

speaker: ORSINO
text: But died thy sister of her love, my boy?

speaker: VIOLA
text: I am all the daughters of my father’s house,

speaker: VIOLA
text: And all the brothers, too—and yet I know not.

speaker: VIOLA
text: Sir, shall I to this lady?

speaker: ORSINO
text: Ay, that’s the theme.

speaker: ORSINO
text: To her in haste. Give her this jewel. Say

speaker: ORSINO
text: My love can give no place, bide no denay.
